failure_modes.text: Agency can become self-blame when people internalize failures caused by real external constraints.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate genuine agency from self-blame by mapping which factors the team can actually influence versus which are externally constrained.

failure_modes.text: False confidence grows when people confuse the feeling of control with actual control over the system.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the feeling of control against actual system dynamics; false confidence grows when perceived control exceeds real leverage.

failure_modes.text: Tunnel vision appears when a person treats a favorite framework as the only controllable answer.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit whether the chosen framework is the highest-leverage available tool or just the most familiar one.

premortem_questions.text: What here is actually under our control, and what must be accepted or redesigned around?
premortem_questions.source_quote: focus on what you can control and not on what you can't

premortem_questions.text: What am I at least one percent responsible for in this outcome?
Did we separate diagnosis from action, or are we moving too fast to feel in control?

heuristics.text: Concentrate effort on controllable levers and stop spending attention on the uncontrollable.
heuristics.source_quote: focus on what you can control

heuristics.text: Use a structured goal-problem-diagnosis-design-do loop to turn agency into action.
Distinguish objective facts from emotions, wishes, and beliefs before acting.
heuristics.source_quote: objective facts and personal emotions, beliefs, wishes, and desires
